description:
You are a web-developer and want a super-quick, local “API smoke-test” that does not touch the public Internet.  
Two minimal JSON documents have already been placed for you inside

    /home/user/simple_api/

The exact file names are:

    /home/user/simple_api/users.json
    /home/user/simple_api/status.json

Goal  
1. With nothing more than curl, read each JSON file via the file:// scheme.  
2. Measure the size of the response body in bytes for each request (⟂ i.e. the exact number of bytes returned by curl, **not** the number of characters reported by wc after you open the file directly).  
3. Create the log file

    /home/user/api_test.log

   in the **exact** format shown below so automated grading can compare it byte-for-byte.

Log-file format (this is the ONLY thing the grader will check)  
• Each line must have three space-separated fields in this order:  
  1) the literal word “GET” (all caps),  
  2) the full file-URL you queried,  
  3) the byte-count returned by curl.  
• One line per endpoint, in the same order given below.  
• **No extra spaces**, tabs, or blank lines.  
• A trailing newline is required at the very end of the file.

Exact template (substitute only the byte-counts you measure):

GET file:///home/user/simple_api/users.json NNN
GET file:///home/user/simple_api/status.json MMM

Where  
NNN = byte-length of users.json obtained through curl  
MMM = byte-length of status.json obtained through curl

Keep the whole task to at most three shell commands.  When you are done, the grader will read /home/user/api_test.log and compare it to the required format, so double-check spacing, ordering and that the file ends with a newline.
truth:
Directory and source data present before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/simple_api/users.json  
   Contents (single line + newline at the end, total 84 bytes including the newline):

{"users":[{"id":1,"name":"Alice"},{"id":2,"name":"Bob"},{"id":3,"name":"Charlie"}]}

2. /home/user/simple_api/status.json  
   Contents (single line + newline, total 38 bytes):

{"status":"ok","uptime_seconds":1234}

The correct byte-counts (as curl will return them):

users.json → 84  
status.json → 38

Therefore the *exact* target content of /home/user/api_test.log (with a trailing newline) must be:

GET file:///home/user/simple_api/users.json 84
GET file:///home/user/simple_api/status.json 38

The automated test will:

• Validate that /home/user/api_test.log exists and is readable.  
• Verify its entire contents match the two-line block above **exactly**, including the final newline.

No other files or network access are required.
name: task_000000_e5678f03
